name: Chocolate Apricot Upside-Down Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Hershey's cocoa or Hershey's European style cocoa
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a 9-inch round or square baking pan, melt the butter in the oven.
3. Remove the melted butter from the oven and add the brown sugar; mix well.
4. Spread the mixture evenly over the bottom of the pan.
5. Arrange the apricots in the pan with the rounded side down.
6. Sprinkle the pecans around the apricots.
7. In a separate bowl, stir together the flour, cocoa (or European style cocoa), baking powder, and salt.
8. In a large bowl, beat the granulated sugar and butter until creamy.
9. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
10. Alternately add the flour mixture, milk, and vanilla extract to the sugar-butter mixture; beat until blended.
11. Spread the batter evenly over the apricots.
12. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
13. Immediately invert the cake onto a serving plate.
14. Serve slightly warm or at room temperature with cinnamon-speckled whipped topping.
Cinnamon-Speckled Whipped Topping:
In a small bowl, combine cold whipping cream, powdered sugar, and ground cinnamon; beat until stiff. About 2 cups topping.
